basic_details.job_title: UPESSC Assistant Professor Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 107 Posts
basic_details.short_information: Uttar Pradesh Shiksha Seva Chayan Aayog (UPSSSC) has released the official Assistant Professor (B.Ed.) Notification 2026 on 28 April 2026 at www.upessc.up.gov.in. A total of 107 vacancies have been announced under the UPSSSC Assistant Professor B.Ed. Recruitment 2026. Eligible candidates aged between 21 to 62 years with a qualification of Postgraduate degree in relevant fields and B.Ed. degree can apply online from 28 April 2026 to 27 May 2026. The official notification PDF and online application link are available below.

application_fee.details: (A) Category-wise Application Fee: | (B) Additional Online Transaction Charges (over and above the application fee): | Note: Candidates from other states belonging to OBC/SC/ST will be treated as General (UR) category candidates and must pay the General category fee. Candidates who previously applied under the original advertisement (dated 09.07.2022 or 23.05.2025) and are re-applying now do NOT need to pay the fee again, provided they hold the required qualification as per Point 6 (mandatory educational eligibility). Their old Application Number has been sent to their registered mobile and email.

age_limit.details: Age Limit: The maximum age of applicants as on 31.08.2022 must not exceed 62 years. Applications will not be submitted if date of birth is not mentioned or the candidate is above 62 years on 31.08.2022. | Pay Scale: ₹15,600 - ₹39,100 with Grade Pay of ₹6,000, along with allowances as permitted by the state government from time to time.

qualification.details: The essential qualifications for the post of Assistant Professor/Assistant Acharya B.Ed. are as follows (as per NCTE Regulations 2014 and UP government order): | Option 1: | Postgraduate degree in Science / Mathematics / Social Science / Humanities with minimum 55% marks AND M.Ed. with minimum 55% marks | OR | Option 2: | Postgraduate degree in Science / Mathematics / Social Science / Humanities with minimum 55% marks AND Postgraduate degree in Education (M.A. Education) with minimum 55% marks AND B.Ed. degree or equivalent with minimum 55% marks | Additionally (for both options): | National Eligibility Test (NET) in Education conducted by UGC | OR | Ph.D. or D.Phil. in Education from a University that has obtained the degree in accordance with UGC (Minimum Standards and Procedure for Award of M.Phil./Ph.D. Degree) Regulations 2009/2016

how_to_apply: Click on the link for Advertisement No. 51 (B.Ed. - Amended)
Register yourself and fill the online application form carefully. Once submitted, no corrections will be made by the commission.
Upload photograph of the prescribed size and sign at the designated place. Applications with incorrect photo or absent signature will be cancelled.
Pay the application fee online through the available payment modes.
The User Manual for filling the online application form (in Hindi and English) is available on the portal. Read it carefully before applying.

Candidates who applied under the previous advertisement (09.07.2022 or 23.05.2025) must re-apply using their old Application Number (sent to registered mobile/email) and need not pay the fee again if they meet the mandatory qualification in Point 6 as on 31.08.2022.
important_instructions: Only candidates who are original domicile residents of Uttar Pradesh are eligible for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/Freedom Fighter Dependent/PwBD reservation benefits. Candidates from other states will be treated as General (UR) category. Key rules include:

Candidates claiming more than one reserved category will be given only one concession — whichever is more beneficial. For OBC reservation, the candidate's parents/family must normally reside in UP and the individual or their family should belong to UP's listed OBC categories. Female candidates' caste certificate based on their husband's caste (for SC/ST) will not be accepted; only the caste certificate based on the father's side will be valid. PwBD (Physically Handicapped) candidates must produce a certificate issued by the competent authority specifying the percentage of disability. EWS candidates must ensure their Income & Asset Certificate is issued in the prescribed format on or before the application closing date. Government employees must obtain a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their employer before applying. The NOC need not be submitted initially but must be produced at the time of interview. Candidates married to a person who already has a living spouse are not eligible unless exempted by the Governor.
UP Assistant Professor B.Ed. Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process
Selection is based on two stages — Written Examination and Interview:

Objective-type question paper covering General Knowledge (60 questions) and Subject-specific questions (140 questions) Total marks: 200 (General Knowledge: 60 marks + Subject: 140 marks; each question carries 2 marks) Total: 30 General Knowledge questions + 70 Subject questions = 100 questions No negative marking The syllabus for the subject-specific test is available on the UPSSSC portal under the "Examination" section
Candidates shortlisted based on written exam scores will be called for departmental interview and assessment
Final Merit: Written Exam (200) + Interview (30) = 230 marks total
Candidates scoring equal aggregate marks in the merit list will be resolved by giving preference to the older candidate (by date of birth).

Preference (Adhimanta) for College Allotment: At the time of interview, shortlisted candidates must submit their online preference/priority order for college allotment. The order of preference given by the candidate is binding. Male candidates must not indicate women's colleges in their preference order; doing so will make them personally responsible for any resulting discrepancy. Female candidates may indicate preference for both types of colleges.

All UP-domicile reserved category candidates (SC/ST/OBC/EWS) must mention their category in the application. SC/ST/OBC candidates must obtain caste certificates in the formats provided in Annexures 1 and 2 of the notification from competent authority by the last date of application. EWS candidates must produce an Income & Asset Certificate as per Annexure 3, issued by District Magistrate, Additional DM, SDM, Tehsildar, or Pargana Magistrate. PwBD candidates must ensure that the certificate specifies the percentage of disability clearly and is in the prescribed format (Annexure 4) issued by the medical board. Freedom Fighter Dependents must have their claim supported by a certificate in the Annexure 5 format. Once the application form is submitted ("Submit" pressed), no changes in category, gender, date of birth, or other details will be permitted. Applications without the prescribed photograph size or signature will be cancelled.

Exam date, venue, and timing will be communicated via SMS, email, newspaper, and the commission's website. Candidates must appear at the allotted exam centre only — no changes in exam centre will be permitted. Candidates must bring a black ball pen to the exam hall and fill OMR sheets with black ball pen only. Other colours of pens, fountain pens, gel pens, sketch pens, pencils, and whitener/correction fluid are strictly prohibited. Visually impaired candidates can avail Scribe facility as per UP government order no. AI0/210789/2022/65-3099/135/2022 dated 08 September 2022. The scribe's educational qualification must not exceed Intermediate level. Candidates using a scribe will be given 40 minutes of extra time. Candidates who need a scribe must register with the commission office at least one week before the exam. Use of unfair means or misconduct during examination will result in cancellation of candidature and debarment from future examinations.
Candidates shortlisted for interview will be informed via SMS, email, and newspaper. At the time of interview, the following original documents along with two self-attested copies must be produced:

High School mark sheet High School certificate Intermediate mark sheet Intermediate certificate Graduation (all year) mark sheets Graduation degree Postgraduate (all year) mark sheets Postgraduate degree B.Ed. degree Ph.D./D.Phil. degree Ph.D./D.Phil. certificate as per UGC Regulations 2009/2016 from the University Registrar NET/SET/SLET certificate JRF certificate (if applicable) D.Litt./D.Sc./LL.D. degree (if applicable) NOC from current employer (for serving candidates) — in original SC/ST/OBC/EWS caste/income certificate (for UP domicile reserved category candidates) PwBD certificate (for UP domicile PwBD candidates) Freedom Fighter Dependent certificate (for UP domicile candidates) Character certificate from last educational institution Two character certificates from respectable persons (recent) Aadhaar / PAN card / Domicile certificate (any one) Affidavit (on ₹10 stamp paper) regarding no pending court case / conviction
General/EWS/OBC candidates: ₹800/- SC/ST candidates (UP domicile): ₹400/- Fee to be paid online at the commission office/portal at the time of interview

faq.question: Q1. What is UPESSC Assistant Professor B.Ed. Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: UPESSC Assistant Professor B.Ed. Recruitment 2026 is a teaching recruitment drive by the Uttar Pradesh Education Service Selection Commission, Prayagraj for 107 posts of Assistant Acharya / Assistant Professor (B.Ed.) in aided non‑government degree colleges of Uttar Pradesh.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in UP Assistant Professor B.Ed. Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 107 vacancies have been announced under UP Assistant Professor B.Ed. Recruitment 2026. These 107 posts are distributed across 41 aided non‑government degree colleges in Uttar Pradesh with category‑wise breakup of GEN 44, OBC 36, SC 20, EWS 7 and ST 0.

faq.question: Q3. What are the important dates for UPESSC Assistant Professor B.Ed. Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application process for UPESSC Assistant Professor B.Ed. Recruitment 2026 starts from 28 April 2026. The last date to submit the online application form and pay the exam fee is 27 May 2026 (up to 11:59 PM), and the written examination is scheduled to be held on 28 July 2026.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility criteria for UP Assistant Professor B.Ed. 2026?
faq.answer: For UP Assistant Professor B.Ed. 2026, candidates must have a Postgraduate degree in Science / Mathematics / Social Science / Humanities with at least 55% marks along with an M.Ed. degree with a minimum of 55% marks as per NCTE Regulations 2014. Alternatively, candidates can have a Postgraduate degree in the relevant subject with 55% marks, a Postgraduate degree in Education (M.A. Education) with 55% marks, and a B.Ed. or equivalent with 55% marks, plus UGC NET in Education or Ph.D./D.Phil. in Education as per UGC 2009/2016 norms.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for UPESSC Assistant Professor B.Ed. Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for UPESSC Assistant Professor B.Ed. Recruitment 2026 is 62 years as on 31 August 2022. If the date of birth is not mentioned in the application form or the candidate is above 62 years on 31 August 2022, the online application will not be accepted by the commission.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for UP Assistant Professor B.Ed. 2026?
faq.answer: For UP Assistant Professor B.Ed. 2026, the application fee is 2000 rupees for General (UR), OBC and EWS category candidates. The fee for Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) candidates is 1000 rupees, and additional online transaction charges will be applicable depending on the chosen payment mode.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for UPESSC Assistant Professor B.Ed. 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process for UPESSC Assistant Professor B.Ed. 2026 consists of two stages – a written examination and an interview. The written exam will be of 200 marks (General Knowledge and subject‑specific questions) and the interview will carry 30 marks, making the final merit list out of a total of 230 marks.

faq.question: Q8. Is there any negative marking in the UP Assistant Professor B.Ed. written exam?
faq.answer: There is no negative marking in the UP Assistant Professor B.Ed. written examination. Candidates will not lose marks for wrong answers, so they can attempt all questions strategically to maximize their score.
